claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 21 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 15 years old and does not live with you.
  - Charlie is 11 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Claimant contributes £50.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 11 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 21, which is 20 or over; Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie. I'd like to know if I'm eligible for Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! You have three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie. Let me ask you some questions about each of them to determine eligibility.

Let's start with some general questions:

**How old are Alex, Blake, and Charlie?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 21, Blake is 15, and Charlie is 11.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now let me ask about each child's situation:

**Is Alex (who is 21) currently in any approved education or training?** 

(Note: Child Benefit normally covers children under 16, or under 20 if they're in approved education or training)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training. He actually works full-time now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying about Alex. Now let me ask about Blake and Charlie:

**Do Blake (15) and Charlie (11) both live with you in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I live in the UK, in Birmingham. Charlie lives with me, but Blake doesn't - Blake lives with their other parent.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to understand Blake's situation a bit more:

**Do you make any financial contributions towards Blake's upkeep, such as for food, clothes, pocket money, or maintenance payments? If so, roughly how much do you contribute per week or month?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do help out with Blake. I contribute about £10 a week - I help with some pocket money and bits and pieces when I can.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me now assess the eligibility for each of your three children based on the Child Benefit rules.

Let me summarize what you've told me:
- **Alex (21)**: Not in education or training, works full-time
- **Blake (15)**: Lives with their other parent, you contribute about £10/week
- **Charlie (11)**: Lives with you in the UK

Based on the rules, I can now provide my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Summary

Based on your circumstances, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie only**.

Here's the breakdown for each child:

**Alex (21) - Not Eligible**
- Alex is over 20 and not in approved education or training. Since Alex works full-time, Child Benefit does not apply.

**Blake (15) - Not Eligible**
- Although Blake is under 16, Blake lives with their other parent. To continue receiving Child Benefit when a child lives elsewhere, you must contribute at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment (£25.60/week for eldest child or £16.95/week for additional children). Your contribution of £10/week falls below this threshold. Additionally, the other parent may already be claiming for Blake, as only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child.

**Charlie (11) - Eligible ✓**
- Charlie is under 16 and lives with you in the UK, so you qualify for Child Benefit for Charlie.

You can proceed to claim Child Benefit for Charlie. If you'd like to explore whether you could increase your contributions for Blake to become eligible, you would need to contribute at least £16.95 per week and ensure the other parent isn't claiming for Blake.

reasonings: Alex is 21 years old and not in approved education or training. Child Benefit normally covers children under 16, or under 20 if they are in approved education or training. Since Alex is working full-time and not in education or training, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 15 and lives with their other parent. While you can continue to receive Child Benefit for a child living with someone else if you contribute to their upkeep, the contribution must be at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment. The current Child Benefit rate is £25.60 per week for the eldest child and £16.95 per week for additional children. Your contribution of £10 per week is below the required threshold, so you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for Blake. Additionally, only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child, and the other parent Blake lives with may already be claiming.

Charlie is 11 years old and lives with you in the UK. You normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. Since Charlie is under 16 and lives with you, you are eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has three children. Alex (21) is not eligible as he is over 20 and not in approved education or training. Blake (15) is not eligible because although Blake is under 16, Blake lives with the other parent and the claimant's contributions (£10/week) are less than the Child Benefit payment rate required to maintain eligibility. Charlie (11) is eligible as Charlie is under 16 and lives with the claimant in the UK. The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for one child only - Charlie.

Child Benefit normally covers children under 16, or under 20 if they are in approved education or training. Since Alex is working full-time and not in education or training, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 15 and lives with their other parent. While you can continue to receive Child Benefit for a child living with someone else if you contribute to their upkeep, the contribution must be at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment. The current Child Benefit rate is £25.60 per week for the eldest child and £16.95 per week for additional children. Your contribution of £10 per week is below the required threshold, so you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for Blake. Additionally, only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child, and the other parent Blake lives with may already be claiming.

Charlie is 11 years old and lives with you in the UK. You normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. Since Charlie is under 16 and lives with you, you are eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has three children. Alex (21) is not eligible as he is over 20 and not in approved education or training. Blake (15) is not eligible because although Blake is under 16, Blake lives with the other parent and the claimant's contributions (£10/week) are less than the Child Benefit payment rate required to maintain eligibility. Charlie (11) is eligible as Charlie is under 16 and lives with the claimant in the UK. The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for one child only - Charlie.
